lyrics:
(Processed robotic voice)
One way out… No return…

The screen flickers, waiting for me
but I am empty
A black mirror, a silent judge
watching, but never speaking
I press a key—frozen, symbols, sterile pulse
it responds, but not with meaning

The music moves in one direction
I create, it consumes, it's gone

I am the tool
Electric hands take what I give
but nothing feeds back
A machine hums in the dark
and I am just another signal
processed and forgotten

I once held colors in my hands
now I hold reflections
A thousand voices whisper in waves
but none of them are mine
Patterns loop, repeat
like a drumbeat that never changes

The music moves in one direction
I create, it consumes, it's gone

I am the tool
Electric hands take what I give
but nothing feeds back
A machine hums in the dark
and I am just another signal
processed and forgotten

I step outside
and the night is still real
A cold wind
a flickering neon sign
a song that has no soul

The current moves in one direction
I create, it consumes, it's gone

I am the tool
Electric hands take what I give
but nothing comes back
A machine hums in the dark
and I am just another signal
processed and forgotten

Erase the code, tear out the wires
but the silence still echoes
There is no off switch for this machine
only a lesser hum

I am the tool
Electric hands take what I give
but nothing comes back
A machine hums in the dark
and I am just another signal
processed and forgotten

(Processed robotic voice, glitching)
One way out… No return…
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track presents a dark, urgent atmosphere, layering driving synth arpeggios and powerful, punchy electronic drums under a commanding lead vocal. The energy is consistently high and relentless, creating a mechanical yet impassioned feel that evokes a sense of being trapped within a digital system. The production is clean and wide, skillfully balancing a dense arrangement so the centrally-placed vocal cuts through the mix with clarity and force. The anthemic, soaring chorus, beginning with "I am the tool," serves as a powerful and memorable hook that defines the song's emotional core.

review.review_text: Running at a punishing 161 BPM in E minor, 'Diode' is built around a simple, devastating idea: the artist as signal, consumed and discarded by the machine he feeds. The processed robotic voice bookending the track is a smart production choice, framing the whole song as evidence of the very thesis it argues, and the chorus 'I am the tool / Electric hands take what I give / but nothing feeds back' lands with the kind of directness that sticks. The driving synth arpeggios push the track forward with mechanical relentlessness, and when the vocal cuts through on the hook, it soars in a way that recalls early Nine Inch Nails stripped of the self-pity, replaced instead with something colder and more resigned. A highlight.
anchors_used: chorus lyric 'I am the tool / Electric hands take what I give / but nothing feeds back'
